Paeonia suffruticosa is a perennial plant native to China, belonging to the Paeoniaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Paeonia suffruticosa include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as paeoniflorin and paeonol. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or ointment to treat various health conditions, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tablets.
